incidents.victim: cPanel / WebHost Manager users (~1.5M instances)
incidents.vector: Authentication-bypass flaw (CVE-2026-41940, CVSS 9.8) injects line-break payloads into the login session file before credentials are verified, allowing unauthenticated takeover.
incidents.impact: Active exploitation observed before a patch was available; CISA added the CVE to its KEV catalog and Namecheap blocked cPanel ports for customers ahead of patching. About 1.5 million cPanel hosts are exposed.

incidents.victim: Microsoft SharePoint Server customers
incidents.vector: Spoofing zero-day (CVE-2026-32201) in SharePoint Server allows attackers to present falsified content within trusted SharePoint environments; one of 167 fixes in April Patch Tuesday alongside publicly disclosed Windows Defender flaw 'BlueHammer'.
incidents.impact: Active in-the-wild exploitation reported. Enables phishing, unauthorized data manipulation, and social-engineering pivots inside enterprise SharePoint deployments. Microsoft urging immediate patching.

incidents.victim: U.S. retail, hospitality, aviation, automotive, financial-services, legal, technology, and academic firms
incidents.attacker: Criminal gang — data extortion (English-speaking, Scattered Spider-affiliated)
incidents.vector: Vishing calls and SMS lures direct employees to adversary-in-the-middle phishing pages cloning corporate SSO/IdP portals; captured tokens grant SaaS-wide access. Attackers swap MFA devices, suppress alert emails, and exfiltrate data through residential proxies (Mullvad, Oxylabs, NetNut, NSOCKS).
incidents.impact: Two extortion crews active since October 2025 are speed-running data-theft attacks on critical-infrastructure-adjacent firms. Demands typically in the seven-figure range; non-paying victims have been hit with DDoS and employee swatting. Data-leak site offline as of this week but campaign ongoing.

incidents.victim: Moldova National Health Insurance Company (CNAM)
incidents.vector: Network intrusion against CNAM's claims and contributions database; specific entry vector not disclosed.
incidents.impact: Up to one-third of CNAM's database — including personal info and payment records for the national insurance system — possibly exfiltrated. No ransom demand received, leading Moldovan officials to suspect a state-sponsored data-theft operation; attribution to Russian-linked actors is being assessed.

incidents.victim: Brazilian regional ISPs (multiple)
incidents.attacker: Criminal gang — DDoS-for-hire (Brazil-based, abusing Huge Networks infrastructure)
incidents.vector: Mirai-variant botnet built from TP-Link Archer AX21 routers vulnerable to CVE-2023-1389 plus open DNS resolvers used for amplification; attacker leveraged stolen SSH keys belonging to Huge Networks' CEO and the firm's IP space to launch attacks.
incidents.impact: An anti-DDoS provider's compromised infrastructure was used for sustained 10-60 second DDoS bursts against small Brazilian ISPs. Huge Networks CEO acknowledges January 2026 intrusion via a bastion host but disputes intent; investigation ongoing with third-party forensics firm engaged.

incidents.victim: Delta Dental Insurance Company / Delta Dental of New York
incidents.attacker: Criminal gang — data extortion (MOVEit zero-day operators)
incidents.vector: Zero-day exploitation of MOVEit Transfer (mid-2023 incident) gave attackers access to file-transfer servers used to exchange consumer data among DDIC/DDNY affiliates and partners.
incidents.impact: NYDFS issued a $2.25M penalty for cybersecurity-program and incident-reporting failures. Names, SSNs, driver's-license numbers, financial-account info, and patient-health information of New York consumers were exfiltrated.

incidents.victim: U.S. and Canadian freight brokers and carriers
incidents.vector: Phishing emails and compromised carrier accounts give attackers access to freight load boards; criminals impersonate brokers, post fraudulent loads, and use 'double-brokering' to redirect shipments. In some cases the FMCSA contact and insurance details of legitimate carriers are altered to expand the scam.
incidents.impact: FBI advisory: ~$725M in cargo stolen across U.S./Canada in 2025, a 60% jump from 2024 with average loss per theft up 36%. Car dealerships, vape companies, and other shippers report repeated million-dollar losses; some victims are extorted post-theft for return of cargo.

incidents.victim: Israeli water and desalination operators
incidents.vector: ZionSiphon ICS-targeting malware checks for Israeli IP ranges and Israeli water-utility software strings, then loads a config that forces water-pump pressure and chlorine levels to maximum; signed by a persona '0xICS' with messages supporting Iran, Palestine, and Yemen.
incidents.impact: Sample analyzed by Darktrace; the malware's geo-check fails due to an XOR coding bug, causing it to delete itself rather than detonate. No confirmed in-the-wild infections, but capability and intent indicate continued Iranian targeting of Israeli water OT infrastructure consistent with attacks since 2020.

incidents.victim: Cuban Embassy, Washington DC
incidents.vector: Email-account compromise (specific initial-access vector not disclosed) inside the embassy's mail environment.
incidents.impact: Researchers at Gambit Security found that intruders read the email of 68 officials, including the Cuban ambassador and deputy chief of mission, beginning January 2026. Campaign coincided with Trump-administration moves against Cuba and Venezuela, including a U.S. naval blockade and oil-delivery halt.

incidents.victim: Vect 2.0 ransomware victims (manufacturing, healthcare, education, technology)
incidents.attacker: Criminal gang — ransomware (allied with TeamPCP supply-chain operators)
incidents.vector: RaaS deployment via TeamPCP's supply-chain-implanted RAT (Trivy, Checkmarx KICS, LiteLLM, Telnyx) and other initial-access vectors. Targets Windows, Linux, and ESXi.
incidents.impact: Check Point found Vect 2.0's ChaCha20-IETF implementation discards three of four nonces for any file >128KB — making large files (VM disks, databases, backups) permanently unrecoverable even with a paid decryptor. Effectively a wiper masquerading as ransomware.

incidents.victim: Sri Lanka Ministry of Finance
incidents.vector: Intrusion into the finance ministry's payment systems; funds were diverted during a foreign-debt repayment scheduled to go to Australia.
incidents.impact: $2.5M stolen via the breach; a separate ~$600K payment to the U.S. Postal Service has also gone unaccounted for. Sri Lanka has asked Australian law enforcement to assist; a fragile economy still recovering from sovereign default now faces fresh scrutiny over financial-controls integrity.

incidents.victim: GitHub Enterprise Server / GitHub.com customers
incidents.vector: CVE-2026-3854 (CVSS 8.7): user-supplied git push-option values were inserted into internal protocol metadata without sanitizing the delimiter character, allowing an attacker with push access to inject trusted internal fields and chain payloads to remote code execution.
incidents.impact: Reported by Wiz via AI-assisted reverse-engineering of closed-source binaries; GitHub patched github.com within two hours and confirmed no exploitation. 88% of self-hosted Enterprise Server instances were still vulnerable at disclosure; admins urged to upgrade.

incidents.victim: Southeast Asian scam-center networks (Cambodia, Burma, Laos)
incidents.attacker: Criminal gang — pig-butchering / cryptocurrency investment fraud
incidents.vector: Romance- and friendship-themed pig-butchering scams direct U.S. victims to fraudulent crypto investment platforms run from compounds operating out of casinos and office parks; many scam-center workers are themselves trafficking victims.
incidents.impact: FBI/Dubai Police/Chinese MPS coordination led to 276 arrests; San Diego prosecutors charged alleged managers and recruiters. Treasury OFAC sanctioned Cambodian Senator Kok An and 28 individuals/entities (Apr 23). The Scam Center Strike Force seized 503 fraudulent web domains and a recruitment messaging app.

incidents.victim: France National Agency for Secure Documents (ANTS)
incidents.vector: Fraudulent access to ANTS' state automated personal-data processing system; specific exploitation method not disclosed publicly.
incidents.impact: A 15-year-old taken into custody April 25; advertising 12-18 million records on cybercrime forums under the alias 'breach3d'. Login credentials, names, emails, birthdates, account IDs — and possibly addresses, phone numbers, and birthplaces — of holders of French passports, ID cards, residence permits, and driver's licenses appear authentic.

incidents.victim: ADT (5.5 million customers)
incidents.vector: Voice phishing (vishing) attack against an ADT employee compromised an Okta single sign-on account, providing access to corporate systems and customer data.
incidents.impact: Extortion group claims data on ~5.5 million ADT customers was exfiltrated. Highlights the continuing trend of identity-provider compromise via vishing as the dominant initial-access vector.

incidents.victim: Bundestag President Julia Klöckner; German Bundestag leadership
incidents.vector: Compromise of Klöckner's Signal account; part of an ongoing Russian campaign targeting Signal and WhatsApp accounts of senior officials.
incidents.impact: Attackers gained access to a sensitive group chat that included Chancellor Friedrich Merz (Merz's own account was not breached). Germany's BfV had previously warned of Russia's extensive secure-messaging-account targeting.

incidents.victim: U.S. Federal Civilian Executive Branch agencies (Cisco ASA / Firepower / FTD users)
incidents.attacker: China — state-suspected espionage group (advanced persistent threat)
incidents.vector: Exploitation of Cisco ASA firmware flaws CVE-2025-20333 and CVE-2025-20362 to deploy 'FIRESTARTER' implant on Cisco ASA, Firepower, and Secure Firewall (FTD) devices — providing remote access and persistence that survives firmware patching.
incidents.impact: CISA published a malware analysis report co-sealed with NCSC-UK and updated Emergency Directive 25-03 with new forensic, hunting, and update requirements after detecting FIRESTARTER on FCEB-managed Cisco ASAs. Patch alone does not evict the actor.

incidents.victim: Multiple targets of China-nexus actors (covert botnet-routing victims)
incidents.vector: Use of large covert botnets built from end-of-life SOHO routers, IoT devices, NAS, and firewalls to relay traffic from on-ramp through traversal to exit nodes near the target. Networks (e.g., Raptor Train, KV Botnet) were operated by Chinese information-security companies including the previously sanctioned Integrity Technology Group.
incidents.impact: Joint advisory from NCSC-UK, CISA, NSA, FBI, DC3, and 11 other allied agencies warns that the majority of China-nexus actors now route operations through covert networks, defeating IP-based blocklists and accelerating IOC extinction. Defensive recommendations focus on baselining edge traffic and zero-trust policies.

incidents.victim: 170+ Ukrainian prosecutors; militaries of Greece, Romania, and Serbia
incidents.vector: Spear-phishing and credential-theft against email accounts of prosecutorial and military targets; campaign linked to a GRU cyber unit.
incidents.impact: Russia accessed prosecutorial mailboxes seeking insight into Ukrainian war-crimes investigations and other inquiries; same campaign breached three NATO/EU-aligned militaries. Identified by Ctrl-Alt-Intel.

incidents.victim: Checkmarx KICS users; Bitwarden CLI consumers
incidents.vector: Second compromise in a month of the KICS vulnerability scanner: malicious Docker images, a tainted GitHub Action, and trojanized VS Code extensions were published. Bitwarden CLI is the first major confirmed downstream victim.
incidents.impact: Repeated breach of a developer-security tool extends a broader supply-chain campaign that has also tampered with Trivy, LiteLLM, and Telnyx PyPI packages and feeds initial access to the Vect 2.0 ransomware affiliate program.

incidents.victim: Vercel and downstream customers (multiple)
incidents.vector: An infostealer infection on a Context.ai (AI vendor) employee gave attackers access to Context.ai's Google Workspace, which was then pivoted into Vercel's environment, where attackers exfiltrated credentials for cloud infrastructure.
incidents.impact: Stolen Vercel data is being auctioned online. Vercel notified affected customers and recommended key/secret rotation. Fallout has expanded to additional customers and third-party systems with continuing disclosures this week.

incidents.victim: Carnival cruise customers (7.5 million)
incidents.vector: Same multi-victim extortion campaign that hit Vercel, Zara, 7-Eleven, Udemy, and Medtronic in 2026; attackers leveraged third-party SaaS access and stolen identity credentials to exfiltrate customer data.
incidents.impact: Personal data of 7.5 million Carnival customers leaked online; one of several major brands implicated in the same campaign now publicly disclosing impact.

incidents.victim: Rockwell Automation OT/ICS users (water/wastewater and broader industrial)
incidents.vector: Iran-aligned cluster (tracked by industry vendors as 'Cyber Av3ngers') installed Rockwell FactoryTalk software on VPS infrastructure to target FactoryTalk-managed environments and Allen-Bradley PLCs. Cortex Xpanse observed Rockwell/Allen-Bradley devices on 5,600 IP addresses globally.
incidents.impact: Marks a shift from prior focus on Unitronics PLCs. CISA released a corroborating advisory April 7. Activity continues as Iran selectively restores internet access following a 47-day national outage; geopolitical tension drives ongoing targeting of Western water and industrial OT.

incidents.victim: Itron (utility metering and grid technology vendor)
incidents.vector: Unauthorized access to internal corporate systems; no technical details released.
incidents.impact: Itron disclosed a security breach discovered April 13; the company says the incident has been resolved and most costs will be covered by cyber insurance. Concerning given Itron's role supplying smart-meter and grid-management infrastructure to utilities globally.
